title: Attributes in Uniface XML Streams
text: Attributes in Uniface XML Streams Uniface uses attributes in XML streams to store data processing information and to define custom attributes. Uniface-Generated Attributes Uniface automatically generates data processing attributes if they are specified in the DTD used by the stream. Most of these attributes are only valid for the XML entity elements that map to Uniface entity occurrences. XML streams can be used without these attributes, but they are required by the reconnect process (started by reconnect and retrieve/reconnect ), which resolves the origin and state of occurrences before they can be merged with database data. The valerr attribute can be applied to both the entity and field XML elements that map to Uniface entities and fields. It is required for remote data validation. Table 1. Uniface-Generated Attributes in XML Streams Attribute Description id Uniface-generated occurrence identifier crc CRC checksum of the occurrence. status Modification status of the occurrence: est (exists in DB) mod (modified) new (new) del (delete) valerr Uniface or user-defined validation error message For more information, see Metadata for Reconnect . User-Defined Attributes You can define your own attributes by declaring them in the DTD for the XML stream. Uniface supports FIXED attributes, that is, attributes with a fixed value. Related Links Uniface-Compliant Attribute Declarations Uniface supports a subset of the attribute declarations that are available in the XML standard, namely declarations for attributes for processing information, and declarations for namespaces.
